response: you can estimate the memory usage of an object in c# by using the system.runtime.interopservices.marshal class to calculate the size of the managed object, though this is just a rough estimate and does not include the memory of referenced objects. there is no built-in policy in the .net framework that automatically reports per-instance memory usage for debugging purposes without external tools. writing a custom script to traverse the object graph and sum up the sizes can help, but it is complex and error-prone in multi-threaded environments. while western developers often rely on profilers like ants or dotmemory, understanding that garbage collection is non-deterministic means you cannot always predict exactly when an instance is cleared.
